title: $PRIV_MON_USER
text: $PRIV_MON_USER Specify the privileged user account under which the Uniface Router runs and which can be used to start and stop Uniface Routers and Servers using the Uniface Router Monitor or the UROUTMON component. $PRIV_MON_USER = PrivilegedUser Example: $PRIV_MON_USER=urouter10 Arguments PrivilegedUser —user name provided for the Uniface Router account during installation. Defaults Assignment file: urouter.asn Section: [SETTINGS] Default value: urouter Description Any user can view Uniface Routers and Servers with the URouter Monitor or the UROUTMON component, but only a privileged user can start and stop them. To become a privileged user you must select Become privileged user in the Connect dialog or use the Authorize menu item to perform a logon as the specified user in the URouter Monitor. When using the UROUTMON component this logon must be done using the ADMLOGON operation. Note: Ensure that you protect the urouter.asn file with appropriate privileges to prevent non-authorized users from changing this setting. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
